title: Contributor Code of Conduct
text: contributors maintainers project, pledge respect people contribute reporting issues, posting feature requests, updating documentation, submitting pull requests patches, activities. committed making participation project harassment-free experience everyone, regardless level experience, gender, gender identity expression, sexual orientation, disability, personal appearance, body size, race, ethnicity, age, religion. Examples unacceptable behavior participants include use sexual language imagery, derogatory comments personal attacks, trolling, public private harassment, insults, unprofessional conduct. Project maintainers right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct. Project maintainers follow Code Conduct may removed project team. Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported opening issue contacting one project maintainers. Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant (http:contributor-covenant.org), version 1.0.0, available https://contributor-covenant.org/version/1/0/0/

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: Install development version GitHub load package:
code: devtools::install_github("CHOP-CGTInformatics/REDCapExploreR") library(REDCapExploreR)

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: Functions retrieve REDCap project need API endpoint project token appropriate API permissions. Store credentials outside version-controlled code, environment variables: examples assume redcap_uri token set. package also includes synthetic objects credential-free exploration.
code: redcap_uri <- Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI") token <- Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN")

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: primary workflows independent. Start function matches review goal.

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: build_codebook() returns structured view project fields, choices, forms, events, repeating configuration, project-level metadata. Use mock_codebook inspect output structure without API credentials. view_codebook() presents available section interactive HTML table: See build_codebook() reference output details available viewing options.
code: codebook <- build_codebook(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  codebook codebook$fields mock_codebook #> <REDCap codebook> #> Project: Mock REDCap Database #> Forms: 3 #> Fields: 11 #> Events: 2 #> Repeating enabled: TRUE head(mock_codebook$fields) #> # A tibble: 6 × 19 #>   field_order form_order form_name  form_label field_name field_label field_type #>         <int>      <int> <chr>      <chr>      <chr>      <chr>       <chr>      #> 1           1          1 demograph… Demograph… record_id  Record ID   text       #> 2           2          1 demograph… Demograph… age        Age at enr… text       #> 3           3          1 demograph… Demograph… enrollmen… Enrollment… text       #> 4           4          1 demograph… Demograph… sex        Sex         radio      #> 5           5          2 follow_up  Follow-up  visit_date Visit date  text       #> 6           6          2 follow_up  Follow-up  response_… Response s… text       #> # ℹ 12 more variables: descriptive_field <lgl>, required_field <lgl>, #> #   identifier <lgl>, choice_count <int>, choices <chr>, validation <chr>, #> #   branching_logic <chr>, event_count <int>, event_names <chr>, #> #   repeating_status <chr>, field_note <chr>, matrix_group_name <chr> view_codebook(codebook)

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: build_record_status_data() summarizes REDCap form completion record. plot_record_status() converts table ggplot heat map similar REDCap Record Status Dashboard. synthetic mock_record_status_data can plotted directly:  See plot_record_status() reference compact mode display customization.
code: status_data <- build_record_status_data(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  plot_record_status(status_data) plot_record_status(mock_record_status_data)

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: build_quality_report() retrieves records project structure, runs general data quality checks, returns findings, summaries, standardized metadata. Use mock_quality_report explore report structure example findings: See quality report article check definitions, output structure, interpretation guidance, core assumptions.
code: report <- build_quality_report(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  report report$summaries$project report$findings mock_quality_report #> <REDCap quality report> #> Records: 3 #> Fields: 11 #> Findings: 7  mock_quality_report$findings |>   dplyr::select(     finding_id,     check,     issue,     severity,     record_id,     field_name   ) #> # A tibble: 7 × 6 #>   finding_id check       issue                    severity record_id field_name  #>        <int> <chr>       <chr>                    <chr>    <chr>     <chr>       #> 1          1 metadata    high_risk_free_text      info     NA        visit_notes #> 2          2 outliers    outside_validation_range warning  C002      age         #> 3          3 outliers    future_date              info     C002      enrollment… #> 4          4 operational incomplete_form_status   info     C0003     demographi… #> 5          5 operational incomplete_form_status   info     C002      follow_up_… #> 6          6 operational incomplete_form_status   info     C002      adverse_ev… #> 7          7 operational incomplete_form_status   info     C0003     adverse_ev…

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: pull_redcap_project() retrieves record structural metadata tables used quality-report workflow. Use custom analysis needs underlying API responses directly. mock_redcap_project top-level structure:
code: project <- pull_redcap_project(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token )  names(project) names(mock_redcap_project) #> [1] "data"                  "metadata"              "events"                #> [4] "event_instruments"     "instruments"           "repeating_instruments" #> [7] "project_info"

title: Getting Started with REDCapExploreR
text: Use quality report article deeper review quality checks report interpretation. Browse function reference complete arguments return values. Explore mock_redcap_project, mock_record_status_data, mock_codebook, mock_quality_report REDCap credentials available.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Store REDCap API URI token outside version-controlled code, environment variables, pass build_quality_report():
code: report <- build_quality_report(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") )

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: default call runs five check groups: missingness, metadata, outliers, operational, consistency. Use checks focused review: Summaries standardized metadata always returned. Project- record-level finding_count values reflect selected check groups. mock_quality_report built default checks thresholds.
code: report <- build_quality_report(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN"),   checks = c("missingness", "metadata") )

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Two arguments control heuristic findings. change underlying REDCap data missing-rate calculations. field, sparse_threshold compared missing_count / record_count across rows eligible missingness assessment. field flagged missing rate greater equal threshold. default 0.95, field 19 missing values among 20 applicable rows flagged. numeric, non-choice field record identifier, outlier_iqr_multiplier defines heuristic range: Values strictly outside range flagged. default multiplier 3 uses wider bounds conventional 1.5 * IQR rule less sensitive moderate extremes. check requires least four observed values nonzero IQR. affect metadata-defined validation ranges future-date findings.
code: lower bound = Q1 - outlier_iqr_multiplier * IQR upper bound = Q3 + outlier_iqr_multiplier * IQR

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: practical review usually follows four steps: Inspect project overview. Count findings check, issue, severity. Filter record-field findings review queue. Join field metadata labels field types add context.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Printing report gives short project-level overview. object list findings, summaries, metadata elements. Begin project summary count findings. shows project size structure alongside types review items found.
code: mock_quality_report #> <REDCap quality report> #> Records: 3 #> Fields: 11 #> Findings: 7  names(mock_quality_report) #> [1] "findings"  "summaries" "metadata" names(mock_quality_report$summaries) #> [1] "project" "forms"   "fields"  "records" "events" names(mock_quality_report$metadata) #>  [1] "fields"                "forms"                 "events"                #>  [4] "event_instruments"     "instruments"           "repeating_instruments" #>  [7] "choices"               "validation"            "branching"             #> [10] "record_id_field"       "source" mock_quality_report$summaries$project #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   record_count raw_row_count field_count form_count event_count #>          <int>         <int>       <int>      <int>       <int> #> 1            3            10          11          3           2 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: repeating_enabled <lgl>, missing_rate <dbl>, #> #   finding_count <int>  mock_quality_report$findings |>   dplyr::count(check, issue, severity, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   check       issue                    severity     n #>   <chr>       <chr>                    <chr>    <int> #> 1 operational incomplete_form_status   info         4 #> 2 metadata    high_risk_free_text      info         1 #> 3 outliers    future_date              info         1 #> 4 outliers    outside_validation_range warning      1

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Use scope separate record-field findings field-level metadata aggregate findings. Event repeat columns preserve context longitudinal repeating projects. Filter record, issue, form, severity create review queue. Severity package-provided prioritization aid, clinical regulatory classification. Join findings field metadata labels field types useful review:
code: record_findings <- mock_quality_report$findings |>   dplyr::filter(scope == "record_field") |>   dplyr::select(     finding_id,     severity,     issue,     record_id,     event_name,     repeat_instrument,     repeat_instance,     field_name,     value,     expected   )  record_findings #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   finding_id severity issue               record_id event_name repeat_instrument #>        <int> <chr>    <chr>               <chr>     <chr>      <chr>             #> 1          2 warning  outside_validation… C002      baseline_… NA                #> 2          3 info     future_date         C002      baseline_… NA                #> 3          4 info     incomplete_form_st… C0003     baseline_… NA                #> 4          5 info     incomplete_form_st… C002      month_1_a… NA                #> 5          6 info     incomplete_form_st… C002      month_1_a… adverse_events    #> 6          7 info     incomplete_form_st… C0003     month_1_a… adverse_events    #> # ℹ 4 more variables: repeat_instance <chr>, field_name <chr>, value <chr>, #> #   expected <chr> mock_quality_report$findings |>   dplyr::filter(record_id == "C002") |>   dplyr::select(     finding_id,     severity,     issue,     form_name,     field_name,     value,     expected,     message   ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 8 #>   finding_id severity issue          form_name field_name value expected message #>        <int> <chr>    <chr>          <chr>     <chr>      <chr> <chr>    <chr>   #> 1          2 warning  outside_valid… demograp… age        999   Between… Field … #> 2          3 info     future_date    demograp… enrollmen… 2999… On or b… Date f… #> 3          5 info     incomplete_fo… follow_up follow_up… Inco… Complete Comple… #> 4          6 info     incomplete_fo… adverse_… adverse_e… Unve… Complete Comple… field_reference <- mock_quality_report$metadata$fields |>   dplyr::select(field_name, field_label, field_type, required_field)  mock_quality_report$findings |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(field_name)) |>   dplyr::inner_join(field_reference, by = "field_name") |>   dplyr::select(     finding_id,     issue,     record_id,     field_name,     field_label,     field_type,     required_field   ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   finding_id issue    record_id field_name field_label field_type required_field #>        <int> <chr>    <chr>     <chr>      <chr>       <chr>      <lgl>          #> 1          1 high_ri… NA        visit_not… Visit notes notes      FALSE          #> 2          2 outside… C002      age        Age at enr… text       TRUE           #> 3          3 future_… C002      enrollmen… Enrollment… text       TRUE

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: findings standardized tibble one row per detected issue. report findings returns empty tibble columns. current findings grouped check.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: checks describe absent data field eligible assessment. identify required values missing specific rows fields whose eligible values almost entirely missing.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Metadata checks inspect project design rather validity individual record values. highlight labels, choice definitions, free-text fields may need review project designer data manager. Yes/True/False fields use implicit REDCap choices, require explicit choice definitions.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Outlier checks combine rules configured REDCap general review heuristics. identify values outside metadata bounds, unusually distant numeric values, dates report built. Choice fields excluded IQR outlier detection even stored codes numeric, codes represent categories rather quantities.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Operational checks review REDCap workflow state rather field values. current check identifies applicable forms marked Complete.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Consistency checks compare stored values field definitions identify contradictory checkbox selections. findings tied either specific record-field value aggregate checkbox pattern.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: summaries provides five views normalized project report findings.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: summaries$project one-row overview: record_count: number distinct record identifiers. raw_row_count: number rows returned records API, including event repeating rows. field_count, form_count, event_count: project structure counts. repeating_enabled: whether repeating configuration returned REDCap. missing_rate: weighted missing fraction across applicable field-row cells. finding_count: total findings selected check groups.
code: mock_quality_report$summaries$project #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   record_count raw_row_count field_count form_count event_count #>          <int>         <int>       <int>      <int>       <int> #> 1            3            10          11          3           2 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: repeating_enabled <lgl>, missing_rate <dbl>, #> #   finding_count <int>

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: summaries$forms reports field counts, required-field counts, weighted missing rates form. summaries$fields provides detailed missingness profile: record_count number applicable rows assessed field. necessarily number distinct records events repeating instruments can contribute multiple rows per record. missing_count observed_count partition applicable rows. missing_rate missing_count / record_count, NA rows applicable. distinct_count counts unique nonmissing values. Project form missing rates weighted across applicable field-row cells; simple averages field-level percentages.
code: mock_quality_report$summaries$forms #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   form_name      field_count required_field_count missing_rate #>   <chr>                <int>                <int>        <dbl> #> 1 adverse_events           3                    2            0 #> 2 demographics             4                    3            0 #> 3 follow_up                4                    1            0  mock_quality_report$summaries$fields |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(missing_rate), field_name) |>   dplyr::select(     field_name,     form_name,     required_field,     record_count,     missing_count,     missing_rate,     distinct_count   ) #> # A tibble: 11 × 7 #>    field_name   form_name required_field record_count missing_count missing_rate #>    <chr>        <chr>     <lgl>                 <int>         <int>        <dbl> #>  1 ae_grade     adverse_… TRUE                      4             0            0 #>  2 ae_related   adverse_… FALSE                     4             0            0 #>  3 ae_term      adverse_… TRUE                      4             0            0 #>  4 age          demograp… TRUE                      2             0            0 #>  5 enrollment_… demograp… TRUE                      2             0            0 #>  6 record_id    demograp… TRUE                      2             0            0 #>  7 response_sc… follow_up FALSE                     1             0            0 #>  8 sex          demograp… FALSE                     2             0            0 #>  9 visit_date   follow_up TRUE                      1             0            0 #> 10 visit_notes  follow_up FALSE                     1             0            0 #> 11 symptoms     follow_up FALSE                     0             0           NA #> # ℹ 1 more variable: distinct_count <int>

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: summaries$records contains one row per record. missing_field_count sums missing applicable field values across record’s events repeat instances. finding_count counts record-specific findings; field-level findings belong individual record. summaries$events counts raw API rows unique event name. empty nonlongitudinal projects.
code: mock_quality_report$summaries$records |>   dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(finding_count), record_id) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   record_id missing_field_count finding_count #>   <chr>                   <dbl>         <int> #> 1 C002                        0             4 #> 2 C0003                       0             2 #> 3 C001                        0             0  mock_quality_report$summaries$events #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   event_name     row_count #>   <chr>              <int> #> 1 baseline_arm_1         3 #> 2 month_1_arm_1          7

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: metadata preserves standardized project structure used checks. tables support project-level review help interpret findings:
code: mock_quality_report$metadata$repeating_instruments #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   event_name    arm_num unique_event_name form_name      custom_form_label #>   <chr>           <int> <chr>             <chr>          <chr>             #> 1 month_1_arm_1       1 month_1_arm_1     adverse_events NA                #> # ℹ 1 more variable: redcap_event_name <chr>  mock_quality_report$metadata$validation #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   field_name      form_name      text_validation_type_or_s…¹ text_validation_min #>   <chr>           <chr>          <chr>                                     <dbl> #> 1 age             demographics   integer                                       0 #> 2 enrollment_date demographics   date_ymd                                     NA #> 3 visit_date      follow_up      date_ymd                                     NA #> 4 response_score  follow_up      integer                                       0 #> 5 ae_grade        adverse_events integer                                       1 #> # ℹ abbreviated name: ¹​text_validation_type_or_show_slider_number #> # ℹ 1 more variable: text_validation_max <dbl>  mock_quality_report$metadata$branching #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   field_name form_name branching_logic         referenced_fields #>   <chr>      <chr>     <chr>                   <chr>             #> 1 symptoms   follow_up [response_score] < '10' response_score

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Different check groups intentionally evaluate different sets rows. table, form status determines whether row can enter check, branching logic determines whether currently hidden field excluded, blank values describes happens row field eligible. “Supported logic” refers subset REDCap branching expressions package can evaluate, described Core Assumptions. Event--form mappings repeating configuration respected whenever check evaluates record data. example, blank field Complete form counts missing supported branching logic shows field. contrast, nonblank malformed value Incomplete form can still produce validation finding, even branching currently hides field.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Missingness evaluated two stages. report first determines whether field assessed exported row, determines whether field observed value. prevents structural blanks events, repeating instruments, branching logic, unfinished forms treated missing data.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Raw REDCap form status determines whether row contributes missingness: form exported <form_name>_complete column, fields assessable rows missing rates NA.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Among rows assessable status, applicability accounts : Event--form mappings longitudinal projects. Repeating event instrument configuration. repeating instrument associated exported row. Supported branching logic determines whether field shown. applicable non-checkbox fields, NA blank whitespace-values missing. missing required field produces required_field_missing. required optional field can produce unexpected_sparse_field missing rate meets sparse_threshold.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: REDCap exports one 0/1 column per checkbox choice. report evaluates missingness parent-field level: One checked (1) choices observed. choices explicitly unchecked (0) also observed. meaningful -selection state. parent field missing every exported choice value NA blank otherwise applicable row. Incomplete form, checkbox exports ignored missingness. Unverified Complete form, -zero state observed, -missing set can contribute sparse-field required-field findings. Checkbox consistency findings redefine -zero state missing: checkbox_no_values_selected one field-level prompt option selected across assessed rows explicit checkbox exports. checkbox_none_with_other record-field warning option labeled “None”, “”, “applicable”, “N/”, “NA”, “None ” selected another option.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: REDCap metadata records validation selected project designer REDCap field configuration UI. text fields, selected validation type optional minimum maximum exported text_validation_* columns. Choice definitions configured REDCap similarly exported field metadata. report compares nonblank raw values user-configured rules. inspects structurally applicable rows regardless whether form Incomplete, Unverified, Complete. Branching logic suppress stored value violates metadata rule.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: invalid_choice_value identifies raw codes absent metadata choices radio, dropdown, Yes/, True/False fields. Yes/True/False use implicit REDCap 0/1 definitions. Checkbox fields excluded. metadata codes identify separate option columns, exported cell contains 0 1 selection state.

what: Why the raw export is used
title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: REDCap choice fields stored code displayed label. Validation must compare stored code codes defined metadata, report requests raw values rather labels. example, dropdown choice stored code 1 remains "1" instead replaced display label. Automatic column type guessing also disabled. prevents column 0 1 codes converted logical FALSE TRUE, longer match REDCap choice definitions. Empty API cells imported missing, literal code "NA" preserved actual value. Fields without explicit radio, dropdown, checkbox definitions produce missing_choice_definition instead invalid-choice finding.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: invalid_validation_format applies text fields supported text_validation_type_or_show_slider_number. report infer validation R classes, field names, labels, observed values. DMY, MDY, YMD suffix controls REDCap’s data-entry display. API exports still use canonical YMD date datetime values. Number validations accept values without leading zero, 0.850 .850. provided, text_validation_min text_validation_max produce outside_validation_range valid numeric, date, datetime, time values outside configured bounds. Either bound can used alone. Malformed values produce invalid_validation_format range future-date findings. Use issue names review metadata-defined validation findings together. mock report contains range violation malformed formats invalid choice codes.
code: validation_issues <- c(   "invalid_choice_value",   "invalid_validation_format",   "outside_validation_range" )  mock_quality_report$findings |>   dplyr::filter(issue %in% validation_issues) |>   dplyr::select(record_id, field_name, issue, value, expected) #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   record_id field_name issue                    value expected          #>   <chr>     <chr>      <chr>                    <chr> <chr>             #> 1 C002      age        outside_validation_range 999   Between 0 and 120

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: numeric_iqr_outlier distribution-based heuristic, REDCap metadata rule. behavior depends sample size, field distribution, outlier_iqr_multiplier. future_date applies valid date datetime fields. compares exported calendar date Sys.Date() captured report runs. date system date flagged; datetime later calendar day . Malformed temporal values handled invalid_validation_format instead.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: checks identify items worth reviewing. establish clinical correctness, protocol compliance, regulatory compliance. info finding can still important, report findings prove data correct. finding_id assigned report built persistent across runs. Use issue REDCap context columns tracking findings externally.

what: Branching logic support is intentionally bounded
title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: branching evaluator supports ordinary field comparisons, checkbox choice references, parentheses, /combinations. valid expression evaluates NA treated shown row. REDCap functions, smart variables, expressions outside supported subset treated applicable rather used hide potentially required value. report produce findings apparently missing branching references event-qualified fields, cross-form fields, smart variables classified reliably bracketed tokens alone.

title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: REDCap record can produce multiple API rows events repeating instances. Project record_count counts distinct records, raw_row_count counts exported rows, field record_count counts applicable rows, event row_count counts exported rows per event. Choose measure matches question asked.

what: Results reflect the current project export
title: Understanding REDCap Quality Reports
text: Descriptive fields removed analysis store record values. Structural metadata retrieval failures reported errors rather silently converted empty project structure. Results reflect records metadata available report built, including token’s project access export configuration.

what: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
title: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
text: REDCapExploreR provides exploratory tools REDCap projects accessed REDCap API. Use build structured codebooks, review form completion record status dashboards, generate general data quality reports.

title: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
text: can install development version REDCapExploreR like :
code: devtools::install_github("CHOP-CGTInformatics/REDCapExploreR")

title: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
text: Store REDCap credentials outside scripts, pass build functions: package includes mock_redcap_project, mock_record_status_data, mock_codebook, mock_quality_report offline exploration. See Get Started overview, function reference complete API documentation, quality report article comprehensive guide quality report checks, output elements, assumptions.
code: library(REDCapExploreR)  redcap_uri <- Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI") token <- Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN")  status_data <- build_record_status_data(   redcap_uri = redcap_uri,   token = token ) plot_record_status(status_data)  codebook <- build_codebook(redcap_uri = redcap_uri, token = token) quality_report <- build_quality_report(redcap_uri = redcap_uri, token = token)

title: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects
text: invite give feedback collaborate us! familiar GitHub R packages, please feel free submit pull request. Please let us know REDCapExploreR fails whatever reason database submit bug report creating GitHub issue. Please note project released Contributor Code Conduct. participating agree abide terms.

what: REDCapExploreR: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects — REDCapExploreR-package
title: REDCapExploreR: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects — REDCapExploreR-package
text: Provides tools explore REDCap projects, summarize project structure, flag common data quality issues data managers.

title: REDCapExploreR: Exploratory Data Quality Tools for REDCap Projects — REDCapExploreR-package
text: Maintainer: Richard Hanna hannar1@chop.edu (ORCID)

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
text: build_codebook() pulls REDCap project metadata API returns structured, tidy codebook intended resemble information users see REDCap UI codebook remaining useful downstream R workflows.

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
code: build_codebook(redcap_uri, token)

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
text: redcap_uri REDCap API URI. token REDCap API token.

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
text: redcap_codebook list elements: fields: one row per REDCap field display-oriented metadata, parsed choice summaries, validation, branching logic, event applicability, repeating status available. choices: one row per parsed choice option, including field choice type. forms: one row per instrument/form. events: one row per REDCap event project longitudinal. event_instruments: one row per event/form mapping available. repeating: repeating instrument/event configuration available. project: project-level summary metadata.

title: Build a REDCap codebook — build_codebook
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ codebook <- build_codebook(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") )  codebook$fields codebook$choices } # }

what: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: build_quality_report() pulls REDCap project API, applies general data quality heuristics, returns findings, summaries, interpreted metadata.

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
code: build_quality_report(   redcap_uri,   token,   checks = c("missingness", "metadata", "outliers", "operational", "consistency"),   sparse_threshold = 0.95,   outlier_iqr_multiplier = 3,   progress = c("auto", "none", "show") )

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: redcap_uri REDCap API URI. token REDCap API token. checks Character vector check groups run. sparse_threshold Number greater 0 less equal 1. field flagged unexpectedly sparse missing rate among applicable rows greater equal value. default 0.95 flags fields least 95 percent missing values. outlier_iqr_multiplier Positive number used define IQR outlier bounds Q1 minus value times IQR Q3 plus value times IQR. Smaller values produce narrower bounds findings. default 3. progress Progress display mode. "auto" enables normally throttled progress updates interactive sessions. "none" suppresses progress. "show" enables progress sessions forces every update render.

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: list findings, summaries, metadata elements. findings includes record, form, event, repeat instrument, repeat instance context available REDCap export. summaries$project$raw_row_count equals nrow() records returned REDCap API. summaries$project$field_count count distinct standardized metadata field names. summaries$fields$missing_rate field-level fraction missing among applicable rows. summaries$records contains one row per record; missing-field count summed across applicable event repeat rows. summaries$forms$missing_rate summaries$project$missing_rate weighted fractions missing across applicable field-row cells. Applicability accounts form availability, event-form mapping, repeating instrument structure, branching logic. Missingness assessed raw REDCap form status column <form_name>_complete 1/Unverified 2/Complete. Status 0/Incomplete handled operational checks contribute missingness. Checkbox parent fields observed choice exports contain explicit values, including -zero -selection state; -missing set choice exports missing. Branching expressions use unsupported REDCap functions smart variables treated applicable required values remain reviewable.

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
text: Current findings$issue values findings$check: missingness required_field_missing unexpected_sparse_field metadata duplicate_field_label missing_field_label missing_choice_definition high_risk_free_text outliers outside_validation_range numeric_iqr_outlier future_date operational incomplete_form_status consistency invalid_choice_value invalid_validation_format checkbox_no_values_selected checkbox_none_with_other invalid_validation_format evaluated text fields supported validation configured text_validation_type_or_show_slider_number. Supported validations include REDCap integer number formats, dates datetimes, times, email addresses, North American Australian phone numbers. Number formats accept decimal values without leading zero. Date datetime values validated REDCap's canonical API export formats, regardless configured data-entry display order. outside_validation_range enforces optional text_validation_min text_validation_max bounds using configured validation type. Unvalidated text fields unsupported validation types assessed. invalid_choice_value applies radio, dropdown, Yes/, True/False fields; checkbox fields retain dedicated consistency checks. Choice text validation checks use structurally applicable form, event, repeat rows regardless form completion status branching logic.

title: Build a REDCap data quality report — build_quality_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ report <- build_quality_report(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") ) } # }

what: Build Record Status Dashboard data — build_record_status_data

text: build_record_status_data() retrieves REDCap records project structure API, returns plotting-friendly tile table similar high-level REDCap Record Status Dashboard.

title: Build Record Status Dashboard data — build_record_status_data
code: build_record_status_data(redcap_uri, token)

title: Build Record Status Dashboard data — build_record_status_data
text: redcap_uri REDCap API URI. token REDCap API token.

title: Build Record Status Dashboard data — build_record_status_data
text: tibble one row per dashboard tile columns: project record ID field, form_name, pct_complete. Longitudinal projects also include event_name, REDCap unique event name.

title: Build Record Status Dashboard data — build_record_status_data
text: REDCap stores form status one *_complete field per instrument. function normalizes status values joins record/form grid built API records, metadata, instrument metadata, event metadata, event-instrument mapping. classic projects, output contains one row per record instrument. longitudinal projects, output contains one row per observed record/event/instrument combination enabled event-instrument mapping. Observed record/event combinations taken API rows, including rows exist repeating instrument exported. Repeating instrument instances summarized tile rather returned separate rows. tile's pct_complete value proportion relevant instances marked complete. Non-repeating instruments evaluated non-repeating rows avoid structural blanks repeating instruments. Completion values returned proportions 1 means relevant statuses complete, 0 means relevant statuses complete, NA means completion status available tile. record identifier column uses REDCap project's record ID field name, project infseq_id record ID field returns infseq_id column. form_name factor ordered REDCap event instrument order. longitudinal projects, form_name uses "Event Label : Form Label" labels available.

title: Build Record Status Dashboard data — build_record_status_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ build_record_status_data(   redcap_uri = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_URI"),   token = Sys.getenv("REDCAP_TOKEN") ) } # }

what: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: plot_record_status() creates ggplot heat map data returned build_record_status_data(). plot designed resemble REDCap Record Status Dashboard remaining standard ggplot object users can extend additional ggplot2 layers.

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
code: plot_record_status(   data,   record_id_field = NULL,   mode = c("standard", "compact"),   low_color = "#D73027",   mid_color = "#FEE08B",   high_color = "#1A9850",   missing_color = "grey95",   tile_color = "white",   tile_linewidth = NULL,   x_label = "REDCap Form",   y_label = "Record ID",   fill_label = "Completion Status",   rotate_x_text = TRUE,   x_text_size = NULL,   y_text_size = NULL,   x_tick_every = NULL,   y_tick_every = NULL,   show_y_title = NULL,   form_label_max = NULL )

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: data dataframe returned build_record_status_data(). record_id_field Optional string naming record ID column. default, function infers column form_name, event_name, pct_complete. mode Plot display mode. Use "standard" default dashboard view "compact" smaller x-axis text, thinner tile borders, truncated long form labels. Compact mode preserves default y-axis labels title unless overridden directly. low_color Color pct_complete = 0. mid_color Color pct_complete = 0.5. high_color Color pct_complete = 1. missing_color Fill color missing pct_complete values. tile_color Border color dashboard tile. tile_linewidth Border width dashboard tile. NULL, value chosen mode. x_label X-axis label. y_label Y-axis label. fill_label Fill legend label. rotate_x_text Whether rotate form labels top axis. x_text_size, y_text_size Optional axis text sizes. x_tick_every, y_tick_every Optional positive integers showing every nth x- y-axis tick label. NULL, axis thinned. arguments work standard compact modes. show_y_title Whether show y-axis title. form_label_max Optional positive integer truncating long form labels. NULL, compact mode truncates labels 35 characters standard mode leaves labels unchanged.

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Record Status Dashboard data — plot_record_status
text: function expects one row per dashboard tile form_name column pct_complete column. record ID column inferred remaining data columns possible. longitudinal output, event_name ignored event labels already included form_name. pct_complete range 0 1, 1 complete 0 incomplete unverified. Missing values shown missing_color.
